name: sidebar
title: Sidebar
description: A nested side navigation for section/subsection wayfinding on government sites. Renders a labelled <nav> landmark wrapping a nested list of links: static SidebarSections, expandable SidebarGroups (built on Collapsible, so each is a native button with aria-expanded), and SidebarItems (Commons Link, optional leading Icon). The current page is marked aria-current="page" with TWO non-color indicators — an inline-start accent bar (border-s, flips in RTL) and heavier weight. On desktop the nav is a persistent rail; below md it collapses behind a SidebarTrigger disclosure button (aria-expanded / aria-controls, Escape closes and returns focus), so it stays operable by keyboard and screen reader.
useWhen: Section and subsection wayfinding for a multi-level government site or a document set (a department landing page with sub-pages)
A persistent left rail on desktop that collapses behind a menu button on small screens
Marking the reader's current page within a nested set of navigation links
avoidWhen: Primary site-wide navigation in the page banner (use Header)
Showing the path to the current page as a trail (use Breadcrumb)
A flat list of a few top-level links with no nesting (use a plain nav with Link)
Switching between panels of content on the same page (use Tabs)

keyboard: Tab moves through the links and group triggers in reading order; Shift+Tab moves back
Enter or Space toggles an expandable SidebarGroup (a native disclosure button with aria-expanded / aria-controls)
On small screens Enter or Space on the SidebarTrigger opens and closes the nav; Escape closes it and returns focus to the trigger
Enter follows a SidebarItem link; the current page carries aria-current="page"
The nav content stays in the DOM when collapsed on mobile, so screen-reader element lists and find-in-page remain predictable
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default (links, group triggers, and the mobile trigger are all min-h-11)
notes: The <nav> landmark is named via aria-label (default "Sections") so multiple navs on a page are distinguishable; a SidebarSection heading names its nested list via aria-labelledby.

Non-color redundancy for the current page: aria-current="page" plus an inline-start accent bar (border-s, mirrors in RTL) and heavier font weight, so it stays distinguishable in Windows High Contrast / forced-colors mode where the accent color is overridden.
Links keep the Commons Link mandatory underline (WCAG 1.4.1); the group trigger is the documented nav-landmark exception and underlines on hover only.
The mobile menu is a disclosure, not a dialog: no focus trap, content stays in the DOM, and md:block makes the nav a persistent rail on wider viewports.
